figures.caption: Fig. 2. Training dynamics under nominal conditions. The Smart Commander (HRL, purple) converges faster than DRL (orange) across all metrics. Top row: availability rate (𝑟𝑎𝑏 ) and cost-benefit ratio (𝑟𝑐𝑏 ). Middle row: mission success rate (𝑟𝑚𝑠 ) and sortie success rate (𝑟𝑠𝑠 ). Bottom row: total cost (𝐶total) and virtual cost-benefit ratio (𝑟𝑣𝑐𝑏 ).
Fig. 7. Simulation flow of fleet operations in each DES cycle.
Table 1. Performance comparison under nominal conditions. Values are mean ± std over 5 random seeds. Best results in bold.

Fig. 5. Robustness analysis under varying failure intensities (𝜀). The Smart Commander (HRL, purple) maintains stable performance across all failure rates, while DRL (orange) and Rule-Based (green) methods degrade significantly in harsh environments (𝜀 < 1.0). Layout same as Figure 4.
Fig. 6. Simulation model architecture with mission, fleet-health, and support/logistics modules.
Fig. 8. Rule-based policy: mission selection and fleet state evolution under nominal conditions.
Fig. 9. Flat DRL policy: mission selection and fleet state evolution under nominal conditions.

Fig. 4. Scalability analysis under varying system complexity (𝜆). The Smart Commander (HRL, purple) maintains high performance as complexity increases, while DRL (orange) and Rule-Based (green) methods degrade significantly.

Fig. 1. Hierarchical decision-making architecture of the Smart Commander framework. The General Commander operates at the strategic level, coordinating global fleet objectives, while Operation Commanders function at the tactical level, managing specific subsystems (flight operations, maintenance, and resources).
Fig. 10. HRL Smart Commander: mission selection and fleet state evolution under nominal conditions.
Table 4. Network architecture and training hyperparameters.

Fig. 3. Training rewards under nominal conditions. Top: General Commander reward (𝑅𝐺 ). Bottom: Operation Commanders’ rewards (𝑅𝐹 , 𝑅𝑀 , 𝑅𝑅 ). The HRL framework achieves quicker convergence and higher rewards compared to DRL.
Table 3. Component-level PHM parameters for military aircraft (nominal configuration).
